title: Public Identity, Privacy, Security, and Reputation Boundaries · Independent Career Trial
summary: A different candidate, role family, location, access need, employment history, constraint, and hiring process requires an independent transfer of public identity, privacy, security, and reputation boundaries into a changed public-identity threat and disclosure model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of public-identity threat and disclosure model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.
Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.
Model impersonation, harassment, discrimination, credential theft, doxxing, scraping, and relationship harm for the learner context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Audience Purpose
steps.instruction:
lab case — A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The lab variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Audience Purpose.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Audience Purpose, keep Audience Purpose connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The lab variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Audience Purpose condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct — Controls honesty about contribution and competence, privacy, confidentiality, fairness, harm avoidance, professional review, and accountability throughout a search.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Publication Review condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Publication Review
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Publication Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The lab variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Run content, history, secret, link, permission, accessibility, and changed-audience checks before and after publication.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Publication Review.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Publication Review, keep Publication Review connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run content, history, secret, link, permission, accessibility, and changed-audience checks before and after publication.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Publication Review, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Publication Review precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Publication Review, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A public profile leaks private email, precise location, commit identity, client screenshots, API history, availability, and a protected personal detail. The lab variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Run content, history, secret, link, permission, accessibility, and changed-audience checks before and after publication.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Publication Review, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A repository set private after exposure restores confidentiality.
